paper_title: Phytoplanktonic Composition and POM Remineralization Dynamics

methods.description: This method involves inducing phytoplankton proliferations with different diatom contributions and collecting particulate organic matter (POM) for degradation experiments.

steps.description: Surface water was divided into ten 15-L plexiglass microcosms and incubated at 22°C with different inorganic nutrient enrichments and a 12:12 h light-dark cycle.
steps.searchable_summary: divide surface water into microcosms and incubate with nutrients

steps.description: Five microcosms were enriched with a nutrient solution to reach final concentrations of 23.1 µM of nitrate and 1.35 µM of phosphate; the NP incubation.
steps.searchable_summary: enrich microcosms with nitrate and phosphate

steps.description: The remaining five microcosms received the same nitrate and phosphate enrichment and an additional silicate solution (19.3 µM final concentration) to enhance diatom growth, the NPSi incubation.
steps.searchable_summary: enrich microcosms with nitrate, phosphate, and silicate

steps.description: Chlorophyll-a concentration was measured daily to monitor the bloom development.
One day after the Chl-a maxima, all five microcosms of each light incubation were mixed and the particulate materials from NP and NPSi were collected by means of filtration (>0.6 µm, ipPORE 142 mm).

methods.description: This method describes the resuspension of collected POM into mesopelagic water and the setup of degradation experiments to study remineralization dynamics.

steps.description: All POM collected from each light incubation was resuspended into 60 L of 0.6 µm-filtered mesopelagic water.
steps.searchable_summary: resuspend POM into filtered mesopelagic water

steps.description: Each treatment was distributed into three 20-L fluorinated carboys; one was used for the initial sampling and the other two served as experimental replicates during the 19 days of dark incubation at in situ mesopelagic temperature (14°C).
steps.searchable_summary: distribute treatments into carboys for incubation

steps.description: One carboy filled with mesopelagic water without any addition of particles was incubated as a control and sampled at the start and end of the experiment.
steps.searchable_summary: set up control carboy with mesopelagic water

methods.description: This method involves fitting a first-order exponential decay model to the degradation kinetics of phytoplankton-derived organic carbon (OC) and comparing decay parameters between treatments.

steps.description: Fit a first-order decay model to the POC and TOC (DOC + POC) pools independently, using least square regressions of concentration vs time.
steps.searchable_summary: fit first-order decay model using least squares regression

steps.description: Statistically compare decay curve parameters between treatments and OC pools using extra sum-of-squares F test.
steps.searchable_summary: compare decay parameters using F test

methods.description: This method describes the procedures for measuring various chemical and biological parameters, including chlorophyll-a concentration, phytoplankton identification, nutrient concentrations, and bacterial abundance.

steps.description: Chlorophyll-a concentration was determined using a Turner Designs fluorometer through the fluorometric method.
Identification and enumeration of phytoplankton was performed using the Utermöhl technique.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and enumerate phytoplankton using Utermöhl technique

steps.description: Concentrations of phosphate, ammonium, silicate, nitrate, and nitrite were determined using flow-through colorimetric methods.
steps.searchable_summary: determine nutrient concentrations using colorimetric methods

steps.description: Abundance of prokaryotes was determined through flow cytometry using a BD FACSCalibur instrument.
steps.searchable_summary: determine prokaryote abundance using flow cytometry
